briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — finesse fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with clear water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: 'The dream scenario for a squarebill in fall is a rocky creek channel swing in the back of a major tributary, with shad active near the surface. It's an efficient bait because bass are moving shallow and will bite it in just about any color of water.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: 'The dream scenario for a squarebill in fall is a rocky creek channel swing in the back of a major tributary, with shad active near the surface. It's an efficient bait because bass are moving sha
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Hackney: 'Go to a 1/0 hook on the 2.5 to deaden the action. A shad does not have aggressive action. You still get the big profile but more natural movement for pressured fish.'
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: The importance of showing bass something they haven't seen or heard — a squarebill that combines attributes of a bladed jig (oscillating metal lip with clicking sound) and a crankbait body covers water and triggers reaction strikes

KVD: 'Fall spinnerbaits: downsize to 3/16oz in clear water with small blades to match young-of-year baitfish. For heavy grass, go bigger with willowleaf blades. To fish fast and deep, put smaller blades on a 3/4oz head — the Scorcher design lets y...

Robertson: Spring crankbait progression -- 1) Prespawn: slow-roll lipless on flats near channel banks in 1-6 feet; 2) Postspawn: big-bodied squarebill on tapering points near spawning flats in under 10 feet; 3) Late postspawn: deep diver following...

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal fall fish pull tighter to cover and deeper structure at 8-18ft. The shad migration pauses but doesn't stop. Focus on the deepest fish in a given creek arm — channel bends and main creek points.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
